candidate_check.description: OL 9 must audit all uses of the chage command.

expected.substring: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chage -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-chage

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that OL 9 is configured to audit the execution of the chage command with the following command:

$ sudo auditctl -l | grep chage
-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chage -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-chage

If the command does not return a line or the line is commented out, this is a finding.

expected_values.substring: -a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chage -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-chage

fix_text_excerpt:
Configure OL 9 to generate audit records upon successful/unsuccessful attempts to use the chage command by adding or updating the following rule in "/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules":

-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/chage -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-chage

The audit daemon must be restarted for the changes to take effect.

Restart auditd:
$ sudo service auditd restart
title: OL 9 must audit all uses of the chage command.
